info.title: Secure MCP Tunnel client control-plane API
info.description: Language-independent HTTP and JSON contract implemented by Secure MCP Tunnel clients. This document intentionally contains only the client-facing metadata, long-poll, and response endpoints.

get.summary: Fetch a tunnel metadata record by id
get.description: Fetch the minimal tunnel metadata used for startup diagnostics. Clients only depend on id, name, and description and must ignore additional fields.

parameters.name: X-Tunnel-Client-Name
parameters.description: Stable client implementation name for diagnostics.

example.name: Production MCP
example.description: Tunnel for the production MCP server

403.description: Tunnel-service error response (403). Stable codes: `tunnel_active_organization_required`, `tunnel_use_forbidden`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

429.description: Tunnel-service error response (429). Stable codes: `rate_limit_exceeded`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

get.summary: Long-poll for pending MCP requests destined for a tunnel client

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Optional maximum number of requests to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Optional maximum number of requests to return

parameters.name: timeout_ms

schema.description: Optional client-requested poll wait timeout in milliseconds
schema.title: Timeout Ms

parameters.description: Optional client-requested poll wait timeout in milliseconds

parameters.name: X-Tunnel-Client-Name
parameters.description: Stable client implementation name for diagnostics.

400.description: Tunnel-service error response (400). Stable codes: `invalid_tunnel_id_format`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

401.description: Tunnel-service error response (401). Stable codes: `tunnel_active_organization_required`, `tunnel_missing_principals`, `tunnel_use_forbidden`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

429.description: Tunnel-service error response (429). Stable codes: `rate_limit_exceeded`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

get.description: Long-poll for pending commands. A 204 response means the poll completed without commands; issue another poll. A 200 response contains every command the client must process. When present, response_timeout is a relative duration for the complete command lifecycle, anchored when the poll response is received.

post.summary: Deliver the final JSON-RPC response for an in-flight tunnel request

parameters.name: X-Tunnel-Client-Name
parameters.description: Stable client implementation name for diagnostics.

parameters.name: X-Tunnel-Shard-Token
parameters.description: Opaque shard token from the polled command. Echo it exactly; do not place it in the JSON body.

parameters.name: X-Client-Request-Id
parameters.description: Optional control-plane request identifier for diagnostics.

400.description: Tunnel-service error response (400). Stable codes: `invalid_channel_format`, `invalid_tunnel_id_format`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

401.description: Tunnel-service error response (401). Stable codes: `tunnel_active_organization_required`, `tunnel_missing_principals`, `tunnel_request_mismatch`, `tunnel_use_forbidden`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

404.description: Tunnel-service error response (404). Stable codes: `pending_request_not_found`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

429.description: Tunnel-service error response (429). Stable codes: `rate_limit_exceeded`. Code details and mitigations are documented in the APIErrorBody.code schema.

post.description: Post the result for one polled command. Echo the command request_id in the JSON body and its shard_token in the X-Tunnel-Shard-Token header. Preserve recognized target JSON-RPC errors, including -32003 and -32004, exactly in resp_json. Only non-protocol failures may synthesize -32603 with optional error.data.tunnel_failure provenance.

BearerAuth.description: Send the tunnel API key as Authorization: Bearer <key>.

commands.title: Commands
commands.description: Pending commands the tunnel client must process from this poll response.

TunnelClientMetadata.description: Minimal tunnel metadata used by a tunnel client during startup. The service may return additional management fields; clients must ignore fields they do not recognize.

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: Logical channel for routing MCP traffic (defaults to main).

resp_json.title: Resp Json
resp_json.description: Final JSON payload (omitted when acknowledging notifications). Preserve recognized target JSON-RPC errors exactly. A synthesized -32603 failure may carry optional provenance at error.data.tunnel_failure; the nested schema is published in x-tunnel-failure-schema while this payload remains runtime-opaque.

x-tunnel-failure-schema.description:
Bounded provenance for a synthesized non-protocol MCP failure.

The response payload remains opaque at the tunnel-service wire boundary. This
model owns the published nested contract and gives readers an opt-in validator
without making provenance mandatory for existing clients.

version.description: Positive schema version; readers must tolerate future versions.
version.title: Version

source.description: Bounded failure source. Readers use known values only and treat unknown values as an unclassified tunnel failure.
source.title: Source

upstream_response_received.description: Whether the target returned an HTTP response before the failure.
upstream_response_received.title: Upstream Response Received

upstream_status.description: Target HTTP error status. Present only for source=target_http when an actual target response was received; omit it for synthesized gateway status.
upstream_status.title: Upstream Status

x-tunnel-failure-schema.title: TunnelFailureProvenance

resp_code.title: Resp Code
resp_code.description: HTTP-style status code describing the upstream MCP response

resp_type.description: Indicates whether the payload contains a JSON response body (JSON-RPC response, JSON-RPC notification), or an ack-only notification/session termination response

type.title: Type
type.description: OpenAI API error category, for example invalid_request_error or server_error.

param.title: Param
param.description: Request parameter related to the error when one is available.

code.title: Code
code.description:
Stable tunnel-service error code when one is available. Generic platform or validation errors can return null or a non-tunnel code.

Known tunnel-service codes:
- `invalid_channel_format`: The MCP channel name contains unsupported characters. Mitigation: Use lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, or underscores in channel names.
- `invalid_json_payload`: The MCP request body could not be decoded as a JSON object. Mitigation: Fix the MCP caller/server to send a valid JSON object payload.
- `invalid_tunnel_id_format`: The tunnel ID path segment is not a tunnel_<32 lowercase alphanumeric> value. Mitigation: Use the exact tunnel ID from Platform tunnel settings in control_plane.tunnel_id, CONTROL_PLANE_TUNNEL_ID, or --control-plane.tunnel-id.
- `missing_mcp_session_id`: An MCP session termination request did not include Mcp-Session-Id. Mitigation: Send DELETE session termination requests with the Mcp-Session-Id header from the active MCP session.
- `oauth_shim_harpoon_call_failed`: The OAuth shim Harpoon call failed or returned an invalid upstream response. Mitigation: Check the Harpoon target URL, upstream MCP server health, and tunnel-client logs.
- `oauth_shim_invalid_target_uri`: An OAuth shim metadata URI was malformed or used an invalid harpoon target label. Mitigation: Use an absolute http(s) URI or harpoon://<label> that matches harpoon.targets[].label.
- `oauth_shim_target_not_found`: The requested OAuth shim target label was not found. Mitigation: Add or correct the matching harpoon.targets[].label entry and restart tunnel-client.
- `oauth_shim_unshimmable_endpoint`: The OAuth shim could not map the upstream metadata endpoint to a supported tunnel route. Mitigation: Configure supported OAuth metadata endpoints and grouped harpoon targets for auth-server metadata.
- `oauth_shim_upstream_timeout`: The OAuth shim timed out waiting for a Harpoon response. Mitigation: Ensure the Harpoon target and upstream MCP server respond before the tunnel timeout.
- `pending_request_not_found`: The pending request was already processed, expired, or never existed for this tunnel. Mitigation: No manual action is needed for isolated races; if repeated, ensure only healthy clients acknowledge requests for this tunnel.
- `rate_limit_exceeded`: The caller exceeded the tunnel-service rate limit for this route or tunnel. Mitigation: Reduce request concurrency or volume and let tunnel-client retry after backoff.
- `request_body_too_large`: The MCP request body exceeded the service byte limit. Mitigation: Send a smaller MCP request body; avoid large inline payloads.
- `rfc9728_www_authenticate_unsupported`: The request attempted an RFC9728 WWW-Authenticate probe flow that tunnels do not support. Mitigation: Use the well-known OAuth discovery endpoints instead of WWW-Authenticate probing.
- `tunnel_active_organization_context_required`: The route requires an active organization context but none was available. Mitigation: Select or pass the intended organization ID before listing principals or managing principal-validation overrides.
- `tunnel_active_organization_required`: The tunnel is organization-backed and the request did not include an explicit organization context. Mitigation: Set --control-plane.organization-id, CONTROL_PLANE_ORGANIZATION_ID, or control_plane.organization_id for tunnel-client.
- `tunnel_client_not_connected`: The tunnel-client is not currently connected to drain requests for this tunnel. Mitigation: Start or restart tunnel-client, then check its /readyz endpoint and logs.
- `tunnel_client_not_seen`: No tunnel-client has polled recently for this tunnel. Mitigation: Start or restart tunnel-client with the matching control_plane.tunnel_id and control_plane.api_key.
- `tunnel_management_permission_required`: The caller lacks tunnel management permission for the active org or workspace. Mitigation: Use an admin key or account with Tunnels Read and Manage for the target org/workspace.
- `tunnel_missing_principals`: The authenticated request did not contain org/workspace principals that can be checked for tunnel use. Mitigation: Use a runtime API key associated with the intended organization or workspace.
- `tunnel_principal_association_unverified`: Tunnel creation or update requested principals whose association could not be automatically verified. Mitigation: Use an automatically verifiable org/workspace/tenant combination or request a reviewed association override.
- `tunnel_principal_limit_exceeded`: A tunnel principal list exceeded the per-kind limit. Mitigation: Reduce each of tenant_ids, workspace_ids, and organization_ids to the documented maximum.
- `tunnel_principal_validation_override_automatically_derivable`: A reviewed principal-validation override was rejected because the association is already automatically derivable. Mitigation: Create or update the tunnel directly; no reviewed override is needed for that principal set.
- `tunnel_principal_validation_override_management_permission_required`: The caller lacks permission to manage tunnel principal-validation overrides. Mitigation: Use a credential authorized for tunnel principal-validation override management.
- `tunnel_queue_full`: The per-tunnel pending request queue is full. Mitigation: Reduce MCP request concurrency or add enough healthy tunnel-client capacity to drain the tunnel.
- `tunnel_request_mismatch`: The response request_id does not belong to the tunnel being acknowledged. Mitigation: Verify the client uses the same control_plane.tunnel_id that received the command.
- `tunnel_transport_unavailable`: Redis master changed during queue-v1 work. Mitigation: Retry if safe; promotion may lose or duplicate work.
- `tunnel_use_forbidden`: The caller does not have tunnel use permission for any default principal. Mitigation: Use credentials for a principal that has access to this tunnel or recreate/connect the runtime in the owning org/workspace.

shard_token.title: Shard Token
shard_token.description: Opaque identifier for the backend shard/worker handling the request

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: Logical channel for routing MCP traffic (defaults to main).

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp indicating when the request was enqueued

response_timeout.title: Response Timeout
response_timeout.description: When present, response_timeout is a relative duration for the complete command lifecycle, anchored when the poll response is received. The value contains one non-negative integer followed by one unit: ns, us, ms, s, m, or h. A valid zero such as '0s' represents immediate expiry at the contract level. An absent or null value retains legacy no-deadline behavior.

command_type.title: Command Type
command_type.description: Discriminator for the command payload type

jsonrpc.title: Jsonrpc
jsonrpc.description: JSON-RPC request body to send upstream

shard_token.title: Shard Token
shard_token.description: Opaque identifier for the backend shard/worker handling the request

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: Logical channel for routing MCP traffic (defaults to main).

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp indicating when the request was enqueued

response_timeout.title: Response Timeout
response_timeout.description: When present, response_timeout is a relative duration for the complete command lifecycle, anchored when the poll response is received. The value contains one non-negative integer followed by one unit: ns, us, ms, s, m, or h. A valid zero such as '0s' represents immediate expiry at the contract level. An absent or null value retains legacy no-deadline behavior.

command_type.title: Command Type
command_type.description: Discriminator for the command payload type
